tldr: Move from Segment's hosted CDP event routing to Apache NiFi, an open-source dataflow platform you self-host. Expect significant architecture work: NiFi is a general-purpose flow engine, not a drop-in CDP, so you must rebuild event capture, routing, and destination delivery as flows.
why_switch: Eliminate Segment's annual MTU commit pricing
Full control over routing, transformation, and provenance
Strong fit when audit and provenance tracking matter
what_to_export: List of sources (web, mobile, server) currently sending events
List of destinations and their configurations
Identity resolution rules and user traits
Workspace users, roles, and SSO configuration
export_method: Use the Segment Config API and the workspace UI to export sources, destinations, the tracking plan, and Functions/transformation code as JSON. Commit those exports to a Git repo to keep a versioned record. Pull representative event payloads from each source to capture the actual schemas that downstream systems depend on. If you have warehouse loading or archives enabled, treat that storage as the historical source of truth so you do not need to backfill old events through the new pipeline. Export destination configurations so you can recreate equivalent flows. Document identity rules, traits, and consent settings. Capture users, roles, and SSO configuration. Inventory all API keys and write keys so they can be rotated and removed once cutover completes. Note Personas/Engage audiences separately since they will not port to a generic dataflow tool. Keep Segment running during the parallel period to avoid data loss.
import_method: Provision Apache NiFi on your infrastructure (on-prem, VM, or Kubernetes) following the Apache documentation. Define a flow per logical pipeline: an ingestion processor (for example, ListenHTTP or a queue consumer) to receive events, transformation processors to map to your canonical schema, and delivery processors to push to each destination (warehouse, SaaS, queue). Note that NiFi does not provide a client-side SDK, so web and mobile event capture must be solved upstream by your own collection layer that posts to NiFi's HTTP endpoint or a message queue. Implement identity stitching and consent enforcement as explicit flow logic. Wire authentication, TLS, and access policies according to your security requirements. Use NiFi's data provenance to validate routing during a parallel period when both Segment and NiFi receive a copy of events. Once row counts and downstream behaviours match, cut over and disable Segment sources, then rotate keys.

concept_mapping.target: Custom event collector + ListenHTTP or queue input
Schema files / record readers and writers
NiFi user policies and access groups

gotchas: NiFi is a general dataflow tool, not a CDP; client SDKs and identity logic must be built
Self-hosting adds responsibility for HA, backups, upgrades, and security hardening
Reverse-ETL style activation needs explicit per-destination flows
Consent and PII handling must be implemented as part of the flow
Personas/Engage features do not have a direct equivalent
Segment annual MTU commit may keep billing active until renewal

email.subject: Notice: ending our Segment subscription within 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving event routing in-house onto a self-hosted dataflow platform and will not be renewing Segment. Please treat this email as our 14-day notice to end the subscription at the next available term and to disable auto-renewal.

Kindly confirm the effective end date, the final invoice, and any data or configuration export options still available to us. We will keep sources active until cutover completes.

Thanks for the partnership.

Best regards,
Your customer
